issue_title: Schema.declared_fields should be set by metaclass
issue_body: Since the deepcopy is going to happen anyway, I think it should be done under `SchemaMeta.__new__  `. This way one could have access to the final fields from the schema class without the need for instantiation.

`fields.Contsant` only works for fields which are already present in the object.
```python
from marshmallow import fields, Schema, pprint

class Test(Schema):
    field = fields.Constant(0)
    field2 = fields.Constant(1)

pprint(Test().load({'field': 1}))
```

I'd expect this code to give me:
```
UnmarshalResult(data={'field': 0, 'field2': 1}, errors={})
```
But this is what I get:
```
UnmarshalResult(data={'field': 0}, errors={})
```

If this is the expected behavior, it isn't really clear from the docs.
So either the docs or the behavior ought to be changed.

Since the `unknown` option now defaults to `RAISE` (version 3.0.0b13), there are libs where exists loading data to schema but not `unknown` parameter.

For instance:
- webargs
  - https://github.com/sloria/webargs/blob/dev/webargs/core.py#L392
  - https://github.com/sloria/webargs/blob/dev/webargs/asyncparser.py#L75
- flask-apispec (use webargs)
  - https://github.com/jmcarp/flask-apispec/blob/master/flask_apispec/wrapper.py#L43
- maybe somewhere else...

@sloria I decided to open single issue here to not duplicate issues across your projects.

issue_title: Callable as `missing` value for a DateTime field not working as expected
issue_body:
With version 3.0.0b7, I am trying use a callable to set a datetime when a field is not set:
```python
class MySchema(Schema):
    f1 = fields.Int(missing=lambda: 123)
    f2 = fields.DateTime(missing=lambda: datetime.datetime(2018, 2, 18))
```
When I am trying to use the schema as following, though, I get a ValidationError:
```python
>>> MySchema().load({})
ValidationError: {'f2': ['Not a valid datetime.']}
```
I would expect to get a dictionary `{'f1': 123, 'f2': datetime(2018, 2, 18, …)`. Since I am new user, I am not sure if this is expected behavior, but when comparing this to the Int field, it looks like a bug to me.

I know that currently the marshmallow-polyfield package exists that adds polymorphism to nested fields. But I'd be very interested in a polymorphic schema, much akin to what is feasible using [SQLAlchemy's joined table inheritance](http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/orm/inheritance.html#joined-table-inheritance)

Would this be something that may be implemented in marshmallow if someone implements it?

issue_title: Update gcloud.storage docs to use 'from gcloud import storage'
issue_body:
Looks like we use `import gcloud.storage` a lot in the storage docs (http://googlecloudplatform.github.io/gcloud-python/latest/storage-api.html).

I'd prefer if we kept it consistent with datastore, to use `from gcloud import storage`.

Thoughts?

Was recently pointed out in https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/issues/903#issue-83467800 that `httplib2.Http` objects are not threadsafe.

@mwitkow-io suggested that using [`urllib3`][1] connection pooling would be useful to them.

Other users may also want to use [Twisted][2] for their transport layer (instead of `httplib2`).

----

Currently (as of 9491053699708cef29cdbefeba65572e8481f067) this is possible in `datastore` because `Connection` takes a standalone `http` object (of any type the user pleases) and the [only place][3] the `http` object is used has a very clear signature

```python
headers, content = self.http.request(uri=..., method=..., headers=..., body=...)
```

----

Related to https://github.com/google/oauth2client/issues/128


[1]: https://urllib3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/
[2]: https://twistedmatrix.com/trac/
[3]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/3e86aaed3c7e192a17d9b790f90a41cc2b7baa6a/gcloud/datastore/connection.py#L84-L86
See https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/pull/1772/files#r63362850
See https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/pull/299#discussion_r19439386 for context.
https://circleci.com/gh/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/3433

issue_title: [Discussion] oauth2client credentials in a post-oauth2client world.
issue_body:
We recently switched `core` to use [`google-auth`](https://google-auth.readthedocs.io) in #2639 and completely removed support for `oauth2client`.

It is possible that some users are explicitly passing `oauth2client` credentials into `Client` constructors, e.g.:

```python
credentials = oauth2client.contrib.gce.AppAppsertionCredentials()
storage_client = storage.Client(credentials=credentials)
```

After `google-cloud-core==0.22.0` is released this code will no longer work. Only credentials from `google-auth` will work. There is a potential that we could break some user code and there may be some valid reasons for sticking with oauth2client in the near-term (user credentials and storage, for example).

`google-auth` offers a way to [`convert`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-auth-library-python/blob/443df66cb6e7960205c1d2108e67220d27408efe/google/auth/_oauth2client.py#L136) credentials:

```python
import google.auth._oauth2client

new_credentials = google.auth._oauth2client.convert(old_credentials)
```

We have two options:

1. Tell users who still want to use oauth2client to use `google.auth._oauth2client` to convert their credentials.
2. Add code in our `Client` base class to automatically convert credentials and issue a warning.

I strongly prefer (1) so that we do not prolong oauth2client's lifetime and so that we can more quickly free ourselves to switch http libraries.

issue_title: Cannot set billingTier in synchronous query
issue_body:
# Thank you for reporting an issue to google-cloud-python!

If you are reporting an issue or requesting a feature, please search the
existing open and closed issues to see if there is already work being done.

- https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues
- http://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/google-cloud-python

If you can provide us with as much of the following information as possible it
will help us identify the cause of your issue more quickly.

This is not bug reporting, but enhancement request.
When user tries to change billing tier, it can be done when they call query with async
job = client.run_async_query('<random_jobID>', QUERY)
job.maximum_billing_tier = 3

but in case of client.run_sync_query. it returns query instead of job.
It cannot change biling tier.
Can we add biling tier parameter in Query class?


1. OS type and version
2. Python version and virtual environment information `python --version`
3. google-cloud-python version `pip show google-cloud`, `pip show google-<service>` or `pip freeze`
4. Stacktrace if available
5. Steps to reproduce

Using GitHub flavored markdown can help make your request clearer.
See: https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/

Reference: https://github.com/daspecster/google-cloud-python/blob/add-storage-snippets/docs/storage_snippets.py

A few questions I have...
1. Should I make a PR to merge in at this point since we're not currently running snippets AFAICT?
2. If we're running these as system tests(which makes sense to me), I'm not sure how to handle things like email addresses for users and other account information([example](https://github.com/daspecster/google-cloud-python/blob/42e40cfc5b1bc8b91ecdcf603ae16485f09618ab/docs/storage_snippets.py#L71)).
3. If I should make mocks instead of hitting the service, it seems like that defeats the purpose unless I can inherit from the real class and just override the methods or some similar strategy?

issue_title: google-cloud-logging does not deserialize log messages
issue_body:
Cloud logging currently does not parse logging proto message payloads. 

This forces items like GAE RequestLogs to appear serialized under ```payload_pb``` as shown below

```
<google.cloud.logging.entries.ProtobufEntry object at 0x7fa6793621d0>

{
'payload_pb': type_url: "type.googleapis.com/google.appengine.logging.v1.RequestLog"
value: "\n\025s~mineral-minutia-820\022\0011\032T5b26e25700ff0a0425ac3c89260001737e6d696e6572616c2d6d696e757469612d383230000131000100\"\013216.9.110.82\014\010\327\304\233\331\005\020\210\341\200\271\002:\014\010\327\304\233\331\005\020\270\316\373\272\002B\005\020\260\355\372\001R\003GETZ\017/js/myjsfile.jsb\010HTTP/1.1h\260\002p_\202\001hMozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/67.0.3396.87 Safari/537.36\242\001\037mineral-minutia-820.appspot.com\320\001\377\377\377\377\377\377\377\377\377\001\330\001\001\262\002\0061.9.54\320\002\001"
,
```

For example the visualization description of a chloropeth map including a hover: https://vega.github.io/vega/examples/county-unemployment/

When I check out the .html that folium creates, I see a ton of `<script>` and `<link>`
 tags that refer to websites that I have never even heard of.

Isn't this a huge security issue of the library if I dynamically access/execute source code from unknown sources on the internet every time that I open the .html that folium has created?
I mean someone could easily alter the source code that I'm accessing / executing dynamically, right?

issue_title: Multiple Vincent graphs in the same Marker Popup
issue_body:
Add a marker on a map, at the location corresponding to my real sensor location.
By clicking on the marker I would like a popup to appear , and show all data channels delivered by this sensor, typically more than 3 and less than 10…
I feel like using the Vincent/Vega functionalities of folium (e.g. folium.Vega() methods) is the way to go.
(cf. your “Vincent/Vega Markers” example at page https://folium.readthedocs.org/en/latest/quickstart.html#markers , but now imagine I would like to have several graphs like the “Dominant wave Period (s)” , one above the other, on the same popup… )
 
I have the impression that adding several Vincent.Line visualization on the same popup is not currently feasible…
Note that using the Vincent.line in the “multiline” mode is NOT an option as my different signals have different timestamps (time reference) and sampling frequency…
 Thank you.

Thanks in advance for your kind support.
Regards,
     Giovanni

Originally reported by: **Oliver Ruebel (Bitbucket: [oruebel](https://bitbucket.org/oruebel), GitHub: [oruebel](https://github.com/oruebel))**

----------------------------------------

A typical analysis is to perform a frequency decomposition (or other form of decomposition of signals), e.g,. of electrical recordings of the brain. This kind of analysis results in a timeseries with an additional dimension to represent the bands/features the signal is decomposed into. A possible target for this is FilteredEphys, but, e.g., the ElectricalSeries does only allow for 2D data.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/lblneuro/pynwb/issue/9

The README appears to be end user focused and the Wiki is empty, so there doesn't appear to be any documentation on how to contribute to the project or to hack on the code.

I can figure out some of the dependencies by looking at the Makefile (e.g. pep8, pylint, 
mypy), but explicit documentation on what developers need to install, what tests should be run before submitting PRs, etc would be useful.

Also, background documentation for things which might not be readily apparent to your garden variety Pythonista such was why it's a good thing to pretend to do static typing in a dynamically typed language, how to install `mypy` (since they don't appear to believe in installation instructions), etc

Beside other fields I have a `DateTimeField` in my datatable.

    ValueError at /history/
    year=122 is before 1900; the datetime strftime() methods require year >= 1900 

Is this a known problem? How can I fix this?
If there is no workaround, how can I exclude this field from searching?
